content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Duff-in-the-Army". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/587/problem/C
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of soldiers. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the heights of the soldiers.
content.outputFormat: The output should be the minimum number of operations required to make all soldiers have the same height.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the heights of the soldiers and find the median. The minimum number of operations is the sum of the absolute differences between each height and the median.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n log n) due to sorting

code.Python3:
import sys
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
arr.sort()
median = arr[n // 2]
sum = 0
for i in range(n):
    sum += abs(arr[i] - median)
print(sum)
